document.title: Post Market Clinical Follow-Up Plan
authorityOrJurisdiction: authorized competent authorities and notified bodies
standards: MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC, Annex X CLINICAL EVALUATION
MEDDEV 2.12/2 rev.2 POST MARKET CLINICAL FOLLOW-UP STUDIES
MEDDEV 2.7.1 & 2.7.2 Rev.3 Guidelines on Medical Devices (Clinical Evaluation)
MEDDEV 2.12-1 Rev.8 Guidelines on Medical Devices (Vigilance system)
EN ISO 14971[2012] Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices
MDCG 2020-7 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Plan Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies

sourceSummary.oneLine: PMCF plan for the Abyz Digital Detector ADD, including HAD1417MCW, under CE MDD clinical evaluation requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co.,Ltd.
keyPoints: The objective is to confirm clinical performance and safety throughout the expected lifetime of the medical device.
The scope is collection of clinical data of ADD in the market after CE marking is acquired.
PMCF activities are planned through device registry and survey in the event of a serious accident on the product.
ADD is a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children, not for mammography.
The product is Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: The document is a Post Market Clinical Follow-Up Plan for Abyz Digital Detector model ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Post Market Clinical Follow-Up Plan According to MEDDEV 2.12/2 Product Name Abyz Digital Detector Model Name ADD File No. A-PMCF-001 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The document number is A-PMCF-001, revision 0, dated 2021.01.28.
evidence.evidenceText: Doc. No. A-PMCF-001 Rev. No. 0 Date 2021.01.28

evidence.claim: The plan is intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co.,Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: The information contained in this Post Market Clinical Follow-Up Plan is intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of the manufacturer H&abyz Co.,Ltd.

evidence.claim: The PMCF objective is to confirm clinical performance and safety, risk acceptability, and emerging risks over the device lifetime.
evidence.evidenceText: The objective is to confirm clinical performance and safety throughout the expected lifetime of the medical device, the acceptability of identified risks and to detect emerging risks on the basis of factual evidence.

evidence.claim: The PMCF scope is collection of clinical data of ADD after CE marking is acquired.
evidence.evidenceText: Collection of the clinical data of ADD in the market after CE marking is acquired.

evidence.claim: The plan cites MDD, MEDDEV, EN ISO 14971, and MDCG 2020-7 requirements and guidance.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is made in accordance with the requirements 1) MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC, Annex X CLINICAL EVALUATION 2) MEDDEV 2.12/2 rev.2 POST MARKET CLINICAL FOLLOW-UP STUDIES 5) EN ISO 14971[2012] Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices 6) MDCG 2020-7 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Plan Template

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co.,Ltd in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Name H&abyz Co.,Ltd Address 1F, 2-dong, 41-16, cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-myeon, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The EU Representative is JaviTech e.K. in Schwalbach am Taunus, Germany.
evidence.evidenceText: EU Representative Name JaviTech e.K. Address Sachsenhausener Straße 16, 65824 Schwalbach am Taunus, Germany

evidence.claim: HAD1417MCW of ADD is a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The HAD1417MCW of ADD is wired or wireless digital flat panel detector that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: ADD is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Intended Population Adult and children except infant and pregnant women

evidence.claim: Contraindications include mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The target disease category is ICD-10 R00-R99 for abnormal findings without diagnosis.
evidence.evidenceText: Target disease ICD-10 Code: R00-R99 Abnormal findings on diagnostic imaging and in function studies, without diagnosis

evidence.claim: The product is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Product class Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC

evidence.claim: PMCF activities are planned through device registry and survey if a serious accident occurs.
evidence.evidenceText: We plan to collect clinical data of post-market with PMCF activities through device registry and survey in the event of a serious accident on our product.

evidence.claim: PMCF activities aim to confirm safety and performance, identify side-effects and emergent risks, maintain benefit-risk acceptability, and identify systematic misuse or off-label use.
evidence.evidenceText: confirming the safety of the medical device - confirming the performance of the medical device - identifying previously unknown side-effects... identifying and analysing emergent risks - ensuring the continued acceptability of the benefit-risk ratio - identifying possible systematic misuse or off-label use of the device

evidence.claim: The PMCF plan references a Clinical Evaluation Report, Risk Management Plan, Risk Management Report, and FMEA Table.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Report (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-060 rev.1, 27.01.20 Risk Management Plan (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-036 rev.0, 10.04.2020) Risk Management Report (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-037 rev.0, 15.06.2020) FMEA Table (Doc.No. A-TCF-5-038 rev.0, 15.06.2020)

evidence.claim: The PMCF evaluation report is planned yearly after serious adverse events or accidents are reported.
evidence.evidenceText: We will conduct Post Market Clinical Follow Up evaluation yearly ever since the serious adverse events or accident of our product is reported.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text supports CE, MDD, MEDDEV, MDCG, competent authorities, and notified bodies rather than FDA.
The PMCF activities summary table is blank in the source text, and activity details are described only as planned future content after a serious accident is reported.
The EU Representative email appears as 'fo@javitech.de' in the source text and may require source verification before reuse.
